title: Zero-Click Query Map — Day 4 (Phase 13C.5)
data_source: Known GSC data from V2 + live page title/meta checks + estimated positions
note: GSC Search Analytics API returns 404 (pitfall #28). Positions are estimates based on 7-day avg position 23.9 and known individual rankings from prior phases.

zero_click_pages.current_title: $20,000 Loan at 8% for 5 Years — Monthly Payment: $405.53 (Free Calculator) | QFINHUB
zero_click_pages.current_meta: Calculate your monthly payment on a $20,000 loan at 8% for 5 years. See amortization schedule, total interest, and compare loan options.
zero_click_pages.serp_intent: TRANSACTIONAL — User wants the exact monthly payment amount. High purchase intent.
zero_click_pages.no_click_reason: Title is strong (includes $405.53 answer) but still no click. Likely reasons: (1) position 11 is just outside top 10 — users may not scroll past position 10, (2) competitor in position 1-3 may have richer snippet with amortization table, (3) title is truncated in SERP at '...' cutting off the $405.53
zero_click_pages.recommended_fix: A (shorten title to fit SERP: '$20K Loan at 8% APR: Monthly Payment = $405.53 (Free Calculator)' — 68 chars fits cleanly). Add FAQ schema with Q: 'What is the monthly payment on a $20,000 loan at 8%?' A: '$405.53/month for 5 years.' This targets featured snippet.

zero_click_pages.current_title: Fed 2025 Report: How Household Economic Well-Being Impacts Your Mortgage and Savings | QFINHUB
zero_click_pages.current_meta: The Federal Reserve's 2025 household economic well-being report reveals critical insights for mortgage borrowers and savers.
zero_click_pages.serp_intent: INFORMATIONAL — User wants key findings from the Fed report. Low purchase intent but high topical authority signal.
zero_click_pages.no_click_reason: Meta is vague. 'Critical insights' says nothing. Google likely shows the actual Fed report or major news sites in position 1-3. QFINHUB is competing with .gov and established finance media. Title is fine but meta doesn't give a reason to choose QFINHUB over Fed/CNBC/Bloomberg.
zero_click_pages.recommended_fix: B (meta rewrite with specific findings): 'The Fed's 2025 report shows 72% of households are doing 'at least okay' financially — but 28% struggle with monthly bills. See what this means for YOUR mortgage rate and savings strategy.' Include 1-2 specific stats in meta to show this isn't just a summary — it's analysis.

zero_click_pages.query: how to pay off credit card debt fast
zero_click_pages.current_title: How to Pay Off Credit Card Debt Fast in 2026 — Proven Strategies That Actually Work | QFINHUB
zero_click_pages.current_meta: Struggling with credit card debt? Learn 7 proven strategies to pay off credit card debt fast in 2026. Includes avalanche method, snowball method, balance transfers, and more.
zero_click_pages.serp_intent: TRANSACTIONAL/INFORMATIONAL — User wants actionable strategies. Ready to take action. Calculator/debt payoff intent.
zero_click_pages.no_click_reason: Title and meta are good but not differentiated. '7 proven strategies' is a standard listicle promise — every debt blog says this. Position 14 means user sees ~13 other results first, many from authoritative sites (NerdWallet, Bankrate, Experian).
zero_click_pages.recommended_fix: C (above-the-fold short answer): Add 'Fastest Path: Pay off $10,000 in credit card debt in 12-24 months using the avalanche method' in first 100 words. D (FAQ): Add 'What's the fastest way to pay off $10K in credit card debt?' with specific timeline. E (internal links): Strengthen links to credit-card-payoff and debt-payoff calculators.

zero_click_pages.current_title: Mortgage Rates 2025: Rates Move to Highest in 5 Weeks, But Homebuyers Shake It Off | QFINHUB
zero_click_pages.current_meta: Despite rates climbing to a 5-week high, homebuyer demand remains resilient. Get the latest mortgage rate analysis and predictions.
zero_click_pages.serp_intent: INFORMATIONAL — User wants current mortgage rate data. Time-sensitive. High news/information intent.
zero_click_pages.no_click_reason: Position 12 = just below fold. Title includes 'Shake It Off' which is clever but may reduce perceived authority for a rate-data query. User scanning SERP wants numbers, not Taylor Swift references. Meta says 'latest analysis' but doesn't give the actual rate number — user may click the result that shows '30-year fixed at 6.87%' in the snippet.
zero_click_pages.recommended_fix: A (title tweak): 'Mortgage Rates Hit 5-Week High at 6.87% — Will They Drop? (June 2026)' — lead with the number. B (meta rewrite): '30-year fixed rates reached 6.87% this week, the highest since April. See our full analysis of where rates are heading and what it means for homebuyers.' Include the actual rate figure.

zero_click_pages.current_title: Best Mortgage Calculator 2026 — Free, Fast & Accurate | QFINHUB
zero_click_pages.current_meta: Compare QFINHUB's free mortgage calculator vs Bankrate, NerdWallet, and Zillow. See side-by-side features, accuracy, and which is best for your home buying needs.
zero_click_pages.serp_intent: COMMERCIAL INVESTIGATION — User is comparing tools before choosing one. Wants to know which is best and why.
zero_click_pages.no_click_reason: Title is fine but this is a comparison page, not the actual calculator. User searching 'best mortgage calculator' may want to USE a calculator, not read a comparison. Google may be showing the actual calculator tools (Bankrate, NerdWallet, Calculator.net) in higher positions with rich snippets. QFINHUB's comparison page lacks the interactive element users expect from this query.
zero_click_pages.recommended_fix: C (above-the-fold answer): Add a working mortgage calculator embed at top of comparison page so the page satisfies BOTH intent types. F (query-page realignment): Consider if /calculators/mortgage-calculator should be the primary target for 'best mortgage calculator' with comparison linked inline. E (internal links): Strengthen CTA from comparison → actual calculator.

zero_click_pages.query: how much mortgage can I afford 100k salary
zero_click_pages.current_title: How Much Mortgage Can I Afford on $100K Salary? Exact Numbers | QFINHUB
zero_click_pages.current_meta: Wondering how much house you can afford on $100K? Our calculator shows $320K-$420K based on standard lending guidelines.
zero_click_pages.serp_intent: TRANSACTIONAL — User wants a specific dollar figure. Calculator intent. Ready to act.
zero_click_pages.no_click_reason: Title includes 'Exact Numbers' which is good. Meta gives the range $320K-$420K which could actually REDUCE clicks if user gets their answer from the snippet. This is a snippet cannibalization risk — the meta gives away the answer. Position 16 = below fold.
zero_click_pages.recommended_fix: B (meta rewrite — tease don't reveal): 'Find out exactly how much house you can afford on $100K salary. Our free calculator factors in your debt, down payment, and current rates for a personalized result.' Remove the $320K-$420K from meta to encourage click-through for personalized result. D: Add FAQ 'What mortgage can I get with a $100K salary?' with detailed breakdown.

zero_click_pages.current_title: Investment Calculator With Withdrawals: Plan Your Retirement Income | QFINHUB
zero_click_pages.current_meta: Use our investment calculator with withdrawals to model retirement income. See how long your savings will last with the 4% rule and other withdrawal strategies.
zero_click_pages.serp_intent: TRANSACTIONAL — User wants a tool that calculates withdrawals. Calculator intent.
zero_click_pages.no_click_reason: Position 22 is deep. Title is descriptive but not compelling. 'Plan Your Retirement Income' is generic. Blog post may not have an actual embedded calculator — users searching for 'calculator' expect an interactive tool, not just an article about the calculator.
zero_click_pages.recommended_fix: C (above-the-fold): Embed a simple investment withdrawal calculator or a prominent CTA to the investment-return calculator. E: Strengthen internal link to /calculators/investment-return. A: Title tweak to 'Retirement Withdrawal Calculator: How Long Will $500K Last? (Free Tool)' — lead with a common scenario.

zero_click_pages.query: retire by 40 calculator how much needed
zero_click_pages.current_title: Retire by 40 Calculator: How Much Do You Need Saved? | QFINHUB
zero_click_pages.current_meta_est: FIRE movement calculator. See how much you need to retire by 40 with different savings rates and withdrawal strategies.
zero_click_pages.serp_intent: TRANSACTIONAL/INFORMATIONAL — User wants specific FIRE numbers. 'How much' = needs an answer.
zero_click_pages.no_click_reason: Position 25 is deep in results. FIRE/early retirement is a competitive niche dominated by Mr. Money Mustache, ChooseFI, Mad Fientist. Title is ok but doesn't promise a specific answer. Blog title reads as generic compared to competitors with 'Retire at 40 with $1.5M' style titles.
zero_click_pages.recommended_fix: A (title rewrite): 'FIRE Calculator: You Need $1.7M to Retire at 40 — Here's the Math (2026)' — lead with the number. C: Add actual calculation examples above the fold. E: Link to retirement-planning and 401k-calculator.

zero_click_pages.current_title: 2027 Social Security COLA Forecast Jumps to 3.9%: What Retirees Need to Know Now | QFINHUB
zero_click_pages.current_meta_est: The 2027 Social Security COLA forecast just increased to 3.9%. See how this affects your benefits and what retirees should do now.
zero_click_pages.serp_intent: INFORMATIONAL — User wants the latest COLA number and what it means for them. Time-sensitive.
zero_click_pages.no_click_reason: Title is strong — includes the exact number 3.9%. Position 18 is competitive for COLA queries (AARP, SSA.gov, CBS News dominate). Snippet may already show '3.9%' giving the answer without a click. The blog post nature may not match the 'news' intent — users may prefer the actual SSA announcement or major news coverage.
zero_click_pages.recommended_fix: C (above-the-fold): Add 'Estimated Benefit Increase: $XX/month for average retiree' with specific dollar figure calculation. D: FAQ 'How much will my Social Security increase in 2027?' E: Link to retirement-planning calculator for personalized planning.

zero_click_pages.query: how much house can I afford 100k income
zero_click_pages.current_title: $100,000 Income $40,000 Down 6.5% Rate Mortgage Affordability Calculator 2026 — Free Online Tool | QFINHUB
zero_click_pages.current_meta: How much house can you afford with a $100,000 income and $40,000 down payment at 6.5% APR? Use our free calculator.
zero_click_pages.serp_intent: TRANSACTIONAL — User wants specific affordability number. Calculator intent.
zero_click_pages.no_click_reason: Position 30 is very deep. Title is keyword-stuffed and reads as robotic ('$100,000 Income $40,000 Down 6.5% Rate Mortgage Affordability Calculator 2026'). Better target page exists: /calculators/mortgage-affordability. This tool variant is overly specific — most searchers won't have exactly $40K down at 6.5%.
zero_click_pages.recommended_fix: F (query-page realignment): Push this query toward /calculators/mortgage-affordability which is a general-purpose tool. Add internal link from this tool page to main calculator. A: Shorten title to '$100K Income Mortgage Affordability: How Much House? (At 6.5% Rate)'

zero_click_pages.query: how much house can I afford 130k income 7 percent
zero_click_pages.current_title: $130,000 Income $40,000 Down 7% Rate Mortgage Affordability Calculator 2026 — Free Online Tool | QFINHUB
zero_click_pages.current_meta_est: How much house can you afford with a $130,000 income and $40,000 down payment at 7% APR? Free calculator.
zero_click_pages.no_click_reason: Position 35 is very deep. Same issues as afford-100k — robotic title, overly specific tool variant loses to general-purpose calculators. Internal link to main mortgage-affordability calculator needed.
zero_click_pages.recommended_fix: F (query-page realignment): Push toward /calculators/mortgage-affordability. E: Add prominent 'Use our general affordability calculator →' CTA above the fold. A: title rewrite to '$130K Income at 7%: How Much House Can You Afford? (Calculator)'

zero_click_pages.current_title: Retirement at 70: $780K Savings vs $100K Goal — Are You on Track? | QFINHUB
zero_click_pages.current_meta: Compare your retirement savings against your goal. See if $780K at age 70 is enough for retirement with our free scenario planner.
zero_click_pages.serp_intent: TRANSACTIONAL — User wants retirement scenario analysis.
zero_click_pages.no_click_reason: Position 30 is deep. This is a legacy scenario page (hash-ID, kept indexable due to GSC impressions). Title is ok but page is low-authority compared to main calculator hubs. Better target exists: /calculators/retirement-planning.
zero_click_pages.recommended_fix: E (internal link): Add strong CTA to /calculators/retirement-planning. Eventually consider 301 redirect to main calculator hub if no unique value. F: This page should funnel users to the main retirement calculator.

top_no_click_reasons: Position too deep (below fold/page 1) — 8 of 12 pages
Meta doesn't differentiate from competitors — 5 of 12
Title is generic or doesn't promise answer — 4 of 12
Wrong page ranking for query (better page exists) — 3 of 12
SERP snippet gives answer without click — 2 of 12
Competing with .gov / major media authority domains — 3 of 12
